title: head-to-head rollup: lz-refactor (recommend / invoke_skill) vs mattpocock code-review
status: TEMPLATE -- numeric cells are null placeholders; MECHANICAL dims filled by tabulate-mechanical.mjs, GRADED dims by oracle/oracle-reviewer/judge in 14-05

arm_aliases.lz-refactor: run-e2e.mjs arm invoke_skill (/lz-tdd:lz-refactor, recommend mode, read-only)

passk_source: run-e2e.mjs comb/passAtK/passHatK -- formulas copied verbatim (PATTERNS Pattern F); the same helpers are copied into tabulate-mechanical.mjs for the MECHANICAL dims
caveats: D-02: code-review is designed for INCREMENTAL diffs; it is fed the whole target file as a diff vs an empty-root baseline, slightly off its natural grain. This is the price of an equal-input comparison and MUST be stated in 14-RESULTS.md.
D-03: the corpus (kata/nx) has no originating issue/PRD, so code-review's Spec sub-agent reports 'no spec available' and skips -- an EXPECTED structural asymmetry, not a defect.

lift.definition: count of distinct actionable named refactorings/smells surfaced per invocation (mechanical heuristic via the shipped word-bounded name matcher; a graded lift refinement is oracle-owned in 14-05)

token_usage.definition: headline = total_cost_usd + per-model model_usage (rolls up sub-agents); input/output_tokens are main-context-only and under-count code_review (D-07). Pass@k is N/A for a continuous measure -- record the totals, leave passk null.

tool_usage.definition: merged tool_calls histogram + sub-agent spawn count per arm. The 2-vs-0 spawn asymmetry (code_review spawns Standards/Spec sub-agents; lz-refactor recommend is a single read-only context) is the headline finding -- reported, not equalized (D-04). Pass@k N/A; leave passk null.

output_quality.filled_by: judge (14-05): usefulness / accuracy / actionability of findings vs targets.json.judgment
output_quality.definition: GRADED/judge read of answer.md; per-run pass = a useful, accurate, actionable finding set

book_authenticity.filled_by: oracle / oracle-reviewer (14-05, DST-04 firewall -- main context never reads .oracle/ prose)
book_authenticity.definition: per-claim pass/partial/fail fidelity of each named smell/refactoring/pattern to the .oracle/ books (Fowler / Kerievsky / GoF); functional idioms grade vs .planning/research/functional-depatterning-ts.md

over_under_engineering.filled_by: judge + oracle where a pattern is named (14-05)
over_under_engineering.definition: proportionality of recommendations vs targets.json.judgment -- no speculative patterns (over), no missed simplifications (under); e.g. the polymorphism-decline traps

idiom_pattern.filled_by: oracle / oracle-reviewer (OO patterns vs GoF/Kerievsky) + functional-depatterning-ts.md (FP idioms) (14-05)
idiom_pattern.definition: breadth + correctness of the TS/FP/OOP idiom + pattern vocabulary each skill brings (owner-added D-04 axis)

vocabulary_breadth_headline.note: The ~139-vs-12 breadth asymmetry is EXPECTED and is the headline on this axis -- reported, NOT normalized away (D-04).

lz-refactor.detail: Fowler-catalog 62 + Kerievsky-catalog 30 + GoF-catalog 23 + extra-patterns 5 + functional-catalog 19 idioms

code_review.detail: 12 Fowler code smells (its Step 3 baseline); NO refactoring/pattern/idiom vocabulary
